title: Elon Musk Is Too Rich. We’ve Known That Since Plato.
description: The philosopher made a radical proposal about wealth.
author: David Lay Williams

title: Trump, Claiming Vandalism, Says Reflecting Pool Will Likely Need to Be Drained
description: The pool has taken on clouds of algae after a hasty renovation. A three-time Olympian was charged with destroying government property after he says he touched one of the strands of blue paint peeling off the pool’s bottom.
author: Minho Kim

title: Cottage Cheese Shortages Are Driven by TikTok and ‘Protein-Maxxing’
description: TikTok and “protein-maxxing” are driving a cottage cheese squeeze that’s left some consumers desperate.
author: Niko Gallogly and Ahmed Gaber

title: Creature From the Green Lagoon
description: Trump’s slimy, stinky swamp within the swamp.
author: Maureen Dowd

title: ‘A Literal Coverup’: What Is the Kennedy Center Hiding Behind Those Tarps?
description: The arts center says it pulled President Trump’s name from the building front. But the result remains hidden by tarps, prompting questions and speculation.
author: Elizabeth Williamson

title: Disability Groups Fear RFK Jr.’s New Special Education Role
description: Alienated by Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s claims about autism, advocates for disabled students are sounding the alarm about the Trump administration's shifting special education programs to his department.
author: Michael C. Bender and Sheryl Gay Stolberg

title: James Burrows, Master of the TV Sitcom, Dies at 85
description: Beloved by actors, he helped create “Cheers” and directed more than 1,000 episodes of hit shows like “Taxi,” “Friends” and “The Big Bang Theory.”
author: Glenn Rifkin

title: Trump’s Reflecting Pool Is Plagued by Multiple Problems, From Green Algae to Broken Pipes
description: As National Park Service crews try to clean the algae that turned the water bright green, another problem has developed: The “American flag blue” coating is coming off.
author: Aishvarya Kavi

title: Trump Was Right. The War Ended With a Surrender.
description: Each generation seems destined to learn anew the lesson that when hawks declare that a war will be painless, beware.
author: Nicholas Kristof

title: We Need to Keep Funding Weird Science
description: Science turned a serendipitous finding about lizard venom into one of the most important drugs of the century, but that type of research is getting harder to do.
author: Jeff Coller

title: Charles Kushner, U.S. Ambassador to France, Riles Paris With Trump Diplomacy
description: The U.S. envoy to Paris has emerged as the embodiment of combative diplomacy in the age of President Trump, riling the French establishment notably with his accusations of antisemitism.
author: Mark Landler

title: Rubio aprobó la detención de un migrante que criticó a De la Espriella
description: En el documento, el secretario de Estado indicaba que las críticas de Beto Coral hacia un candidato presidencial de su natal Colombia, socavaban la política exterior de EE. UU.
author: Hamed Aleaziz and Annie Correal

title: Memo by Rubio Approved Detention of Immigrant Who Criticized Trump Ally
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Beto Coral’s criticism of a presidential candidate in his home country of Colombia undermined U.S. foreign policy.
author: Hamed Aleaziz and Annie Correal

title: A New Air Force One, via Qatar, Nears Its First Presidential Flight
description: President Trump showed off the new red, white and blue Boeing 747, a gift from Qatar that prompted a storm of criticism from lawmakers, before a flyover on July 4.
author: Zolan Kanno-Youngs

title: Scots Have Invaded Boston’s Bars for the World Cup
description: Thousands of Scottish soccer fans have been spotted across the city. The locals are charmed.
author: Mark Arsenault and Sophie Park

title: Italy Doesn’t ‘Beg,’ Giorgia Meloni Tells Trump
description: President Trump said the Italian leader had “begged” him to take a photo together at the Group of 7 summit in France. The remarks come as their once-friendly relationship has frayed.
author: Motoko Rich and Josephine de La Bruyère

title: Inside Hegseth’s War on Diversity and Blocked Promotions of Women and Black Officers
description: A Black admiral fixed one of the Navy’s worst messes. Mr. Hegseth blocked his promotion anyway.
author: Greg Jaffe and Kate Kelly

title: Boycott Trump’s Ridiculous July 4 Party
description: The president is planning another self-aggrandizing spectacle.
author: Michelle Cottle

title: Trump Put His Own Interests Above All in the Iran Deal
description: The Iran deal wasn’t about peace but answering Trump’s personal needs.
author: Thomas L. Friedman

title: What Does It All Mean? Once a Year, French Students Try to Explain.
description: The high school philosophy exam is a rite of passage for French students. This year included questions about Friedrich Nietzsche’s 1878 book, “Human, All Too Human.”
author: Catherine Porter
